headline: Meta rolls out a new AI creator assistant on Facebook | TechCrunch

article_items.title: Meta rolls out a new AI creator assistant on Facebook | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and "What are people saying in my comments?"
article_items.excerpt: Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and "What are people saying in my comments?"
article_items.summary: Launched last year, the idea behind the feature is to allow creators to reach more audiences by breaking down language barriers. Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant Hackers hijacked Instagram accounts by tricking Meta AI support chatbot into granting access ‘What a joke’: GitHub Copilot’s new token-based billing spurs consternation among devs I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful Microsoft under fire for threatening security researcher. Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and "What are people saying in my comments?" More from TechCrunch Meta rolls out a new AI creator assistant on Facebook Meta announced on Thursday that it’s introducing a new AI creator assistant on Facebook that will give creators. Meta plans to add new capabilities and bring the assistant to more countries in the future. The new assistant is rolling out to creators in the U.S., Canada, and India.

Launched last year, the idea behind the feature is to allow creators to reach more audiences by breaking down language barriers.

Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant Hackers hijacked Instagram accounts by tricking Meta AI support chatbot into granting access ‘What a joke’: GitHub Copilot’s new token-based billing.
Meta plans to add new capabilities and bring the assistant to more countries in the future.
Meta also announced that it’s introducing new languages for AI translations on Facebook, including Arabic, Bahasa Indonesian, French, Thai, and Vietnamese.

Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and "What are people saying.
With AI-translated Reels , a creator’s tone and sound are preserved and automatically translated into another language.
Prior to joining the publication in 2021, she was a telecom reporter at MobileSyrup.
Beyond performance, the AI assistant can help brainstorm ideas for new content by drawing on what’s trending.
The answers they receive will be based on their own presence and what they can do differently to improve performance.
By giving creators access to an AI assistant, Meta is looking to keep creators active on Facebook as it competes for their attention against rivals like TikTok and YouTube.
You can contact or verify outreach from Aisha by emailing aisha@techcrunch.com or via encrypted message at aisha_malik.01 on Signal.

article_items.title: Designing the hf CLI as an agent-optimized way to work with the Hub
article_items.description: We’re on a journey to advance and democratize artificial intelligence through open source and open science.
article_items.excerpt: We’re on a journey to advance and democratize artificial intelligence through open source and open science.
article_items.summary: We introduced agent-mode output in hf v1.9.0 and have been migrating the rest of the CLI to it gradually in the following releases. Agents are becoming real users of the Hub: they train models, build and clean datasets, and ship demos as Spaces, almost always on behalf of a person. Separately, the commands that move real data take a --dry-run that shows exactly what they'll transfer before they run, which proves handy for humans and agents alike, since neither has to commit to a long download or blind sync: Discoverable, predictable commands hf is built to be probed: run hf to see the resource groups, run --help on the one you need, and every --help ends with real, copy-pasteable examples (which an. These are early numbers (we only began attributing agent traffic in April 2026), but the scale is already significant, and we expect it to keep growing as coding agents become a standard way to work with the Hub. The hf CLI (and the huggingface_hub Python SDK it's built on) detects when a coding agent is driving it by.

The hf CLI (and the huggingface_hub Python SDK it's built on) detects when a coding agent is driving it by.
key_points: AI agent traffic on the Hub We started tracking agent usage of the Hub in April 2026.
We introduced agent-mode output in hf v1.9.0 and have been migrating the rest of the CLI to it gradually in the following releases.

The hf CLI (and the huggingface_hub Python SDK it's built on) detects when a coding agent is driving it by reading the environment variables agents set: CLAUDECODE / CLAUDE_CODE for Claude Code, CODEX_SANDBOX for Codex.

These are early numbers (we only began attributing agent traffic in April 2026), but the scale is already significant, and we expect it to keep growing as coding agents become a standard way to work with the Hub.
(self-report error = the agent reported success on the 17 solvable tasks but the Hub said otherwise.

Each task goes to a fresh coding agent with exactly one way to talk to the Hub: curl / the Python SDK : no hf CLI at all, so the agent falls back to curl against the REST API or the huggingface_hub Python library.
We ran the whole thing twice, on the two most popular coding agents ( Claude Code with Sonnet 4.6 and OpenAI Codex with GPT-5.5).
Benchmarking the hf CLI for Coding Agents To find out whether the hf CLI is really more efficient for agents, we measured it.
Agents are becoming real users of the Hub: they train models, build and clean datasets, and ship demos as Spaces, almost always on behalf of a person.

Each task/tool combination is run 10 times , since coding agents are non-deterministic, about 520 runs per agent (18 tasks × 3 tools × 10 reps, minus a cap on one billable Jobs task) and ~1,000 graded runs in total.
We found that on complex, multi-step tasks the no-CLI baseline (an agent hand-rolling curl or the Python SDK) uses up to 6× as many tokens as the hf CLI.

If your agent interacts with the Hugging Face Hub, we recommend giving it the hf CLI: Then hand it the skill, so it knows the whole command surface from the first turn: Then point your agent at the Hub and let it work.

Our harness provides the model with user instructions and the precise schema of the underlying retrieval index, and it propagates them to all the subsequent stages of query and filter generation, reranking, and answer.

We evaluate Instructed-Retriever-1 on a large-scale internal dataset representative of Knowledge Assistant usage, measuring whether the two scaling mechanisms introduced above improve retrieval quality: parallel query.
The resulting model supports both query and filter generation, as well as verification-style retrieval capabilities, enabling the two stages that make parallel test-time scaling useful in practice.
We trained Instructed-Retriever-1 as a single retrieval-specialized model that supports parallel query generation and reranking.
Instructed-Retriever-1 powers both stages with a single retrieval-specialized model trained for search generation and evidence ranking.
We described how this can be achieved in our earlier Instructed Retriever blog , and we use the same search harness in training our Instructed-Retriever-1 model.
Instructed-Retriever-1 is a single model trained for both retrieval stages: query generation to increase recall and reranking to increase precision, run in parallel to keep latency low.
3 We evaluated the BF16 and FP8 models on KARLBench across 10 trials.
Training Instructed-Retriever-1 Parallel test-time scaling for search requires a model that can do two things well: generate effective searches and judge retrieved evidence.
Claude Sonnet 4.5 and Instructed-Retriever-1 score 80.1 and 81.0 nDCG@10, respectively.
For training Instructed-Retriever-1, we take a different route: rather than scaling compute sequentially, we parallelize it during the initial search phase.

article_items.title: You can now use Ask Gemini in Drive to rummage through your Gmail - Engadget
article_items.description: Slogging through emails to find stuff can be a chore, but Google AI subscribers can now get Gemini to do it.
article_items.excerpt: Slogging through emails to find stuff can be a chore, but Google AI subscribers can now get Gemini to do it.
article_items.summary: Google's "Ask Gemini in Drive" feature announced in March can now use Gmail as a source, just as it already can with Drive folders and files. That means you can ask it questions about Gmail threads spanning emails, files and folders, "to ensure the most helpful and accurate answers possible," Google explained in its Workspace Updates blog . To employ the new feature, you must be subscribed to Google AI Pro or Ultra, or be a Business or Enterprise user. article_items.title: Google’s Gemma 4 12B Shows AI Race Moving to Edge Devices
article_items.description: The new open source model is encoder-free and can run on a 16GB RAM computer.
article_items.excerpt: The new open source model is encoder-free and can run on a 16GB RAM computer.
article_items.summary: It is the latest in the Gemma 4 family of models Google introduced in April. Google’s Gemma 4 12B Shows AI Race Moving to Edge Devices The model, released under the Apache 2.0 license, is another example of how cloud providers are enabling enterprises to run models on local devices for agentic workflows. Instead, they pay a one-time cost for the model itself or download it in the case of Gemma 4 12B, since the model is free for any user with a 16GB laptop. “That’s very powerful to enhance UX and provide richer response and feedback to the user, which means enterprises can deploy smarter devices,” Su continued. Google DeepMind’s introduction of Gemma 4 12B indicates that the future of agentic AI is shifting more toward local PCs and less toward the cloud, underscoring that the AI race is still in early stages and the market's direction remains fluid. Google’s AI lab released the open source model on June 3.
Gemma 4 12B is under the Apache 2.0 license, which gives enterprise developers flexibility and digital control in using, modifying and deploying the model commercially without licensing barriers.
